proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (age_years) is associated with progression-free survival (pfs_months) in this cohort.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps 0 vs 1 vs 2) is associated with shorter pfs_months — ECOG 2 patients have lower mean PFS than ECOG 0.
Stage IV patients (stage_iv=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than non-stage-IV patients.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(age, PFS)=0.7661, p=0.00e+00
PFS female vs male: mean PFS 4.308 vs 4.314 (diff -0.006 mo, t-test p=7.70e-01, n=22371/27629)
PFS stage IV vs not: mean PFS 3.703 vs 5.055 (diff -1.352 mo, t-test p=0.00e+00, n=27481/22519)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) are associated with shorter mean pfs_months than left-sided tumors.
KRAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than KRAS-wildtype.
NRAS-mutant patients (nras_mutation=1) have different mean pfs_months than NRAS-wildtype.
BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than BRAF-wildtype.
MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have different mean pfs_months than MSS patients.
HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) have different mean pfs_months than non-amplified.
NTRK-fusion patients (ntrk_fusion=1) have different mean pfs_months than NTRK-fusion-negative.

PFS ntrk_fusion+ vs -: mean PFS 4.351 vs 4.311 (diff +0.040 mo, t-test p=7.86e-01, n=251/49749)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher alkaline phosphatase is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher AST is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ALT is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher total bilirubin is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher creatinine is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher BUN is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Sodium is associated with pfs_months.
Potassium is associated with pfs_months.
Calcium is associated with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab=0 (overall, ignoring biomarker status).
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab=0 (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib=0 (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0 (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib=0 (overall).

PFS treatment_regorafenib on vs off: mean PFS 5.089 vs 4.117 (diff +0.972 mo, t-test p=1.71e-216, n=10022/39978)

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves mean pfs_months in RAS/BRAF wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0); cetuximab patients have higher PFS than non-cetuximab patients within this subgroup.
treatment_cetuximab improves mean pfs_months in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0).
treatment_cetuximab is harmful or neutral in KRAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation=1).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_cetuximab and right_sided_primary on pfs_months (cetuximab benefit differs by sidedness).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and kras_mutation on pfs_months (cetuximab benefit reduced in KRAS mutant).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and braf_v600e on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: cetuximab effect in RAS/BRAF wildtype: mean PFS 4.444 vs 4.489 (diff -0.045 mo, t-test p=1.70e-01, n=7691/17633)

cetux × kras_mutation interaction coef=+0.020, p=6.65e-01
cetux × braf_v600e interaction coef=-0.026, p=8.05e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) vs no pembrolizumab in the same subgroup.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months.
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in right-sided tumors (right_sided_primary=1) more than in left-sided.
There is an interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and braf_v600e on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: pembro effect in MSI-high: mean PFS 4.299 vs 4.292 (diff +0.007 mo, t-test p=9.57e-01, n=396/2117)

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_encorafenib improves mean pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e=1) vs no encorafenib in the same subgroup.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_encorafenib and braf_v600e on pfs_months.

Within BRAF V600E mutant patients, the combination of treatment_encorafenib and treatment_cetuximab produces additional pfs_months benefit beyond either alone (positive 3-way interaction encor × cetux × braf).

analyses.result_summary: encorafenib effect in BRAF V600E mutant: mean PFS 3.979 vs 4.108 (diff -0.129 mo, t-test p=3.29e-01, n=242/2030)

encor × cetux × BRAF V600E coef=+0.438, p=2.37e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) vs no trast+tuc in the same subgroup.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and her2_amplified on pfs_months.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients who are also RAS/BRAF wildtype (her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0).

treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in patients with ECOG 2 (ecog_ps=2) vs no regorafenib.
treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in stage IV patients (stage_iv=1) vs no regorafenib.
There is a treatment_regorafenib × ecog_ps interaction on pfs_months.

treatment_bevacizumab improves mean pfs_months in stage IV patients (stage_iv=1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, sidedness, biomarkers, key labs, and other treatments, treatment_regorafenib remains independently associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjustment, age_years is independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect: positive).
After adjustment, ecog_ps is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, stage_iv is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, kras_mutation is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, braf_v600e is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, albumin_g_dl is independently associated with longer pfs_months.
After adjustment, cea_ng_ml is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_cetuximab has no main effect on pfs_months (coefficient ~0).
After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_encorafenib has no main effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has no main effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_bevacizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS PFS model R²=0.859, n=50000. Key coefficients: age=+0.176 (p=<1e-300), ecog_ps=-1.171 (p=<1e-300), stage_iv=-1.362 (p=<1e-300), kras=-0.354 (p=<1e-300), braf=-0.357 (p=3.05e-79), right_sided=-0.287 (p=7.92e-273), albumin=+0.469 (p=<1e-300), cea=-0.005 (p=1.85e-97), weight_loss=-0.076 (p=<1e-300), cetuximab=-0.011 (p=0.188), bevacizumab=+0.003 (p=0.734), pembrolizumab=+0.000 (p=0.982), encorafenib=+0.006 (p=0.63), trast+tuc=+0.026 (p=0.0676), regorafenib=+0.943 (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite risk score combining low albumin, high LDH, high NLR, high CRP, and weight loss is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: composite risk score vs PFS Pearson r=-0.1069, p=6.73e-127

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months — KRAS mutation suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
There is a treatment_regorafenib × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months — right-sided primary suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
There is a treatment_regorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months — BRAF V600E mutation suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
Interactions of cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab+tucatinib with their canonical biomarkers are NOT statistically significant on pfs_months.
Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between cetuximab × kras_mutation on pfs_months.
Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between pembrolizumab × msi_high on pfs_months.
Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between encorafenib × braf_v600e on pfs_months.
Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between trast+tuc × her2_amplified on pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Conditional average treatment effects (CATEs) from a T-learner regression tree show that treatment_regorafenib has substantial positive heterogeneity (q90 - q10 wide and centered well above 0 for many patients), implying a strong responder subgroup.

CATEs for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib are centered near zero with narrow spread, implying no strong responder subgroup detectable on this feature set.

analyses.result_summary: T-learner CATE (depth-3 trees, min_samples_leaf=200) per treatment on pfs_months. treatment_cetuximab: mean CATE=-0.009 mo, q10=-0.274, q90=+0.026; treatment_bevacizumab: mean CATE=+0.001 mo, q10=-0.002, q90=+0.166; treatment_pembrolizumab: mean CATE=+0.001 mo, q10=-0.342, q90=+1.036; treatment_encorafenib: mean CATE=-0.017 mo, q10=-1.177, q90=+0.248; treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib: mean CATE=-0.009 mo, q10=-0.242, q90=+0.093; treatment_regorafenib: mean CATE=+0.937 mo, q10=-1.089, q90=+2.669.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The complete responder subgroup for treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is defined by ALL THREE predicates (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0); within this subgroup treatment_regorafenib increases mean pfs_months by approximately 2.7 months relative to no regorafenib, and the effect collapses to ~0 if any one of these predicates is unfavorable.

FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_cetuximab — even in the canonical (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) subgroup the effect on pfs_months is null/very slightly negative.
FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab — even in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) the effect on pfs_months is null.

FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_encorafenib — even in BRAF V600E-mutant patients with cetuximab (braf_v600e=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1) the effect on pfs_months is null.

FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib — even in HER2-amplified RAS/BRAF wildtype patients (her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0) the effect on pfs_months is null.
FINAL: treatment_bevacizumab has no detectable benefit on pfs_months in any subgroup tested (right-sided, KRAS-mutant, stage IV).

analyses.result_summary: Within the candidate subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0): regorafenib increases PFS by +2.758 mo (n_t=3461, n_c=13911, p=<1e-300). Outside the subgroup the effect collapses: KRAS=0/BRAF=0/right=1 diff=+0.083 (p=0.119); KRAS=0/BRAF=1/right=0 diff=-0.204 (p=0.119); KRAS=1/BRAF=0/right=0 diff=+0.061 (p=0.169). Multivariable-adjusted regorafenib coefficient inside subgroup = +2.735 mo (p=<1e-300, n=17372). Multivariable interactions: regorafenib×kras_mutation coef=-1.784 p=<1e-300; regorafenib×braf_v600e coef=-1.769 p=<1e-300; regorafenib×right_sided_primary coef=-1.474 p=<1e-300.
cetuximab in RAS/BRAFwt + left-sided: mean PFS 4.617 vs 4.684 (diff -0.067 mo, t-test p=1.25e-01, n=5067/11376)
pembro in MSI-high: mean PFS 4.299 vs 4.292 (diff +0.007 mo, t-test p=9.57e-01, n=396/2117)
encor in BRAF V600E: mean PFS 3.979 vs 4.108 (diff -0.129 mo, t-test p=3.29e-01, n=242/2030)
trast+tuc in HER2+ RAS/BRAFwt: mean PFS 4.454 vs 4.440 (diff +0.014 mo, t-test p=9.66e-01, n=59/702)
bev adjusted overall coef=+0.006, p=4.49e-01
